CVX.reasoning: CVX has bounced to exactly the wiki-identified short-entry zone (~$192), but the breakout strategy requires conditions that are not met. Consolidation range: stock has been ranging between $181.76 (support, 12 tests) and the $192 area (wiki short-entry), with a recent high of $205.48 and low of $177.74. Today's daily close of $192.22 is at the top of this bounce zone, but volume ratio is only 0.77x the 20-day average — well below the 1.5x required for breakout confirmation. No bullish breakout signal is valid without volume. Daily Bollinger Band width of 0.1157 is not compressed; BB is not in a squeeze, meaning no coiled-spring energy. Daily OBV is trending DOWN and diverging from price — a bearish warning confirming distribution. ADX daily is strong (32.24) but minus_di (29.97) exceeds plus_di (21.31), confirming the underlying trend is bearish. Hourly RSI shows bearish divergence at 76.18 (overbought) while price pushed to hourly upper BB (pct_b 0.92). The wiki context (Apr 11) specifically flagged $192 as the short-entry target on a bounce in a confirmed downtrend, with $199 stop and $178 target. Current data corroborates this: price has arrived at $192 zone on weak volume, OBV diverging, hourly RSI bearish divergence firing. This is a failed-breakout-watch scenario. Confidence is moderate-low (42) because the squeeze is not fired (BB width not compressed), and the bearish signal is more of a trend continuation than a pure breakout trade — this is at the edge of my mandate. If price closes back below $188.50 (hourly support, 16 tests) on elevated volume, the bearish breakdown becomes more clearly in my wheelhouse.

WMT.reasoning: WMT has been the key breakout watch per the wiki: multiple prior runs (Apr 11, Apr 15) flagged the $129 resistance as the critical trigger, explicitly stating the setup condition is 'a close above $129 on 1.5x+ volume.' The consolidation range is well-defined: support at $121.37 (13 tests), resistance at $128.49 (23 tests) and $129, forming a base approximately $7-$8 wide. Range height measured-move target: $129 + $8 = ~$137. Today's hourly price at $131.93 shows price has pushed through the $129 and $128.49 resistance on the hourly timeframe, with hourly ADX strong (30.25, plus_di 36.33 vs minus_di 14.64) and hourly volume at 1.4x average — close to but not at the 1.5x requirement. However, the DAILY close of $128.01 has NOT confirmed a close above $128.49 resistance. Daily volume is only 1.0x average — far short of the 1.5x requirement. Daily ADX remains weak at 12.89 (well below 25), consistent with prior runs' concern about trend strength. The daily EMA stack is aligned bullish (ema_aligned_uptrend: true), and OBV is trending up without divergence. Daily BB width of 0.0733 is moderate — not a full squeeze. Hourly BB pct_b at 0.83 shows price pressing toward the upper band without a close beyond it yet. The bullish setup is tantalizingly close: the hourly is acting well, but daily close confirmation is still needed. Calling bullish at low confidence (38) because: (1) hourly breakout is in progress without daily confirmation; (2) volume on both timeframes is below the mandatory 1.5x threshold; (3) daily ADX too weak for a strong trend. Wait for a daily close above $129 on volume 1.5x+ to increase conviction. Stop placed below the $125.91 hourly support (recent low, 2 tests).

CVX.reasoning: CVX presents a high-conviction short setup at a precisely identified exhaustion zone. Three of five agents are bearish (mean_reversion, breakout, catalyst_news); the two neutrals (trend_momentum, macro_context) both explicitly decline to go long rather than disputing the bearish read. The convergence is textbook: price has rallied from the $183-184 low to exactly the $192-193 resistance zone flagged in the April 11 wiki analysis as the short-entry target. That prior prediction is being validated in real time. Key technical evidence stacking: (1) hourly RSI-21 at 76.18 with confirmed bearish RSI divergence — price made a new hourly high while RSI rolled over; (2) hourly Bollinger %B at 0.92 — price stretched near the upper band; (3) daily OBV is trending DOWN and diverging from price across all agents — distribution, not accumulation; (4) daily ADX 32.24 with -DI (29.97) > +DI (21.31) — the underlying daily trend is bearish despite the bounce; (5) 21-day ROC at -8.78% — this is a bounce inside a downtrend, not a reversal; (6) the bounce occurred on only 0.77x average volume — no institutional accumulation conviction. The catalyst_news agent adds the most time-sensitive pressure: Q1 2026 earnings are imminent (April 30-May 1), insider Pate sold $8.6M with no offsetting buys, and the oil ceasefire has already compressed the energy risk premium. Selling into an overbought, low-volume, earnings-eve bounce with bearish divergence and insider distribution is the playbook. Confidence capped at 58 — the macro_context agent raises a legitimate caveat (Iran port blockade has introduced a new geopolitical oil premium and the prior ceasefire bear thesis is partly invalidated at the macro level), and the daily Z-score is not at a statistical extreme. The R/R math from macro_context (1.85:1 on the long side) reinforces the bearish lean by showing the upside is capped. Synthesized levels cluster tightly: entry median ~$193.0, target median ~$183.5 (three agents cite $181.76-$188.16 — use $183.5 as middle ground), stop median ~$196.0 where all three bearish agents agree the trade is wrong.
CVX.key_conflicts: The central conflict is macro_context vs. the three bearish technicals/catalyst agents. macro_context argues that the Iran port blockade has introduced a new upside regime for oil names — Goldman raised Brent to $90/bbl — which would partially falsify the wiki's bearish thesis (built on a ceasefire collapsing the oil risk premium). This is a genuine disagreement: if Iran escalates into a full blockade, CVX could rally through $200+ and invalidate the short. However, macro_context itself does NOT flip bullish — it calls neutral because the technicals do not confirm a long entry (R/R fails 2:1 minimum, OBV still bearish). The productive read: the Iran escalation is a tail risk that should widen the stop rather than flip the trade. trend_momentum stays neutral because the daily EMA stack is tangled (EMA10 below EMA21) and the 21d ROC is -8.78% — it cannot find a clean long signal, which reinforces the bear. No agent makes a bullish case; the debate is between 'short now' and 'wait for more clarity.' Given that the catalyst_news agent identifies a live earnings event as a near-term catalyst and the hourly RSI divergence is confirmed, the three bearish agents' timing call is appropriate. The macro veto check: macro_context's regime read is 'neutral' not 'bullish' — it declines to veto the bearish signals. Short is the correct call.

WMT.reasoning: WMT is a setup-in-progress, not a confirmed trade. The vote is 2 bullish (breakout, catalyst_news) vs. 3 neutral (trend_momentum, mean_reversion, macro_context) — insufficient consensus to issue a directional trade. However, this is not a coin-flip neutral: both bullish agents agree on direction and the neutrals are blocking on process (R/R math, ADX threshold, confirmation requirements) rather than disagreeing on the fundamental thesis. The bull case is coherent and building: daily EMA stack fully aligned uptrend, hourly ADX 30.25 with +DI (36.33) far above -DI (14.64), hourly breakout above the $129 resistance that wiki analysis identified as the critical trigger, hourly volume at 1.4x average, OBV trending up on hourly with no divergence, analyst consensus $135-137 with Guggenheim raise to $137, pre-earnings positioning window open with May 21 earnings 15 days out, and the consumer-stress/trade-down macro thesis intact in a tariff environment. The blocks are legitimate: (1) daily ADX at 12.89 is critically below the 20-25 minimum — trend_momentum correctly disqualifies on this alone; (2) R/R at current prices (macro_context calculates 0.74-0.96:1 using intraday $131.93 as entry) does not meet the 2:1 bar; (3) the pullback-to-Fib entry that mean_reversion identified has already played out — the current price is back near the swing high, not at an attractive entry; (4) Walton family sold $4.6B — a distribution signal that warrants weight even amid the analyst bullishness. The net read: WMT wants to break out, the hourly chart is leading the daily, but the daily confirmation has not arrived. The trade requires a daily close above $129-130 on volume 1.5x+ with daily ADX inflecting above 20. If that occurs in the next 1-2 sessions, the breakout and catalyst agents' entries ($128.50-$129.50) and targets ($135-$137) become actionable with a stop below $125.50.
WMT.key_conflicts: The key tension is breakout and catalyst_news calling bullish while trend_momentum, mean_reversion, and macro_context all call neutral — and the source of neutrality differs across agents, making it three different flavors of 'not yet.' trend_momentum is blocked by ADX 12.89 (hard process rule: ADX < 20 disqualifies any trend trade regardless of EMA alignment). mean_reversion notes the Fib-pullback entry has already played out and the current price offers poor R/R with price near the swing high, not the support. macro_context runs the asymmetry math and finds R/R below 1:1 at intraday prices — a quantitative veto. Against these blocks, catalyst_news and breakout both point to the hourly breakout above $129 as a live trigger event, the analyst consensus $136 target, and positive pre-earnings positioning dynamics. The productive signal from this disagreement: WMT is at a daily confirmation inflection point. The hourly is leading, the daily needs to confirm with volume and ADX expansion. This is a 'watch and enter on confirmation' situation, not a 'trade now' situation. Walton insider selling ($4.6B) is the one factor that cuts across all agents — both bullish agents acknowledge it — and it properly limits confidence even in the bull scenario.
WMT.watch_condition: Daily close above $129-130 on volume >= 1.5x 20-day average AND daily ADX inflecting above 20. If triggered, entry ~$129.50, target $136.00, stop $125.50 (R/R ~1.7:1 — acceptable given pre-earnings catalyst window).
WMT.timeframe: conditional — trigger expected within 1-3 sessions if setup matures

CVX.reasoning: Macro regime is risk-on (S&P 500 at all-time high, April best month since 2020), but the CVX-specific picture is a direct thesis collision. The prior wiki short thesis (ceasefire collapses oil, target $178) has been falsified by the current macro: the Iran port blockade is now the dominant left-tail risk, Goldman raised Brent to $90/bbl, and no ceasefire is priced. That reverses the fundamental bear case — but the technical picture does not yet confirm a clean bull entry. ADX daily at 32.24 shows strong trend, but -DI (29.97) exceeds +DI (21.31), indicating the directional bias remains bearish despite the price bounce from $184 to $193. Daily OBV is trending DOWN and diverging from price — a distribution signal. The daily MACD just registered a bullish crossover (histogram +0.007) after an extended bearish run, which is encouraging but early. RSI-7 at 80.7 is overbought short-term; hourly RSI-21 at 76.18 also extended with a confirmed bearish divergence on the hourly. Volume is below average (0.77x) on the bounce — no institutional accumulation conviction. Relative performance: ROC 21d at -8.78% means CVX has significantly underperformed the market over the past month. ROC 5/10d at +3.17/+3.95% is a short-term bounce, not a trend reversal. Asymmetry math: entry ~$193.34, nearest major resistance $214.71 = +11.1% upside; stop at nearest support $181.76 = -5.99% downside. R/R = 1.85:1, which falls below the 2:1 minimum bar. Capital preservation: no leverage concerns for an integrated major; balance sheet is sound. However, the Iran escalation risk cuts both ways — if the blockade escalates, CVX benefits on oil price (bull thesis), but broad risk-off de-risking could still drag the stock lower. Regime continuity is unclear for this specific name: the macro winds have shifted to favor energy bulls via Iran, but the technicals show a stock still in distribution after a 21d -8.78% decline. Pass on both long and short: thesis reversal is not yet confirmed technically, and R/R fails the minimum threshold on the long side. A clean bull entry requires the stock to reclaim its SMA-50 ($192.64) with volume confirmation and OBV turning up on the daily.

WMT.reasoning: Macro regime is risk-on (S&P 500 new all-time high, Dow +1.5%, April +9% MTD). WMT fits the consumer-stress defensive rotation theme: tariff pass-through and higher-for-longer rates push consumers to trade down to Walmart's everyday-low-price model. The wiki thesis explicitly identifies this as the 2026 bull case, and the macro environment supports it. However, the technical setup and R/R math block a swing entry at current levels. Daily price $128.01, hourly $131.93 (significant intraday move on Apr 30). The stock recently failed at $132.46 recent high and has been distributing: close sequence over the last 5 days is 132.03 → 129.92 → 127.59 → 127.59 → 128.01 — four days of selling before today's intraday bounce. Daily ADX is weak at 12.89 with -DI (24.94) > +DI (20.82), indicating no strong directional trend and slight bearish daily bias. RSI-14 at 46.79 is neutral. MACD histogram barely positive at +0.045. Hourly picture is more constructive: ADX-24 at 30.25 strong, +DI (36.33) well above -DI (14.64), RSI-21 at 70.29, volume at 1.4x average with OBV trending up — institutional accumulation signal on hourly timeframe. EMA stack fully bullish on daily (ema_aligned_uptrend = true). The positive longer-term structure (all EMAs stacked, +11.67% above EMA-200, +14.62% above SMA-200) is intact, but the swing setup lacks a clean entry. Asymmetry math using the intraday price of $131.93 as proxy entry: upside to volume-confirmed resistance $134.41 = +1.88%; Fibonacci 1.272 extension $136.39 = +3.38%. Downside to hourly support $125.91 = -4.56%. Best-case R/R is 3.38/4.56 = 0.74:1 at the intraday level — well below the 1.5:1 minimum bar. Using daily close basis $128.01: target $134.41 = +5.0% upside, stop $121.37 = -5.2% downside = 0.96:1 R/R. Either way, risk-reward fails. ROC 5d at -1.52% shows slight underperformance vs market this week. ROC 21d +3.65% shows the stock has participated in the April recovery but not led. Capital preservation: WMT balance sheet is solid (Aaa-equivalent mega-cap), no leverage concerns. The 52x earnings multiple flags valuation risk if the trade-down thesis disappoints on tariff cost pass-through. Conclusion: the bull macro thesis is correct for WMT, but the stock needs to digest its recent distribution and the entry R/R does not meet the 2:1 threshold. Wait for a pullback to $125-126 hourly support or a confirmed daily breakout above $134.41 with volume before establishing a swing position.

CVX.reasoning: Branch A fires at the hourly level — partial fade-the-extreme setup. However, the hourly picture is notably stretched: RSI-21 at 76.18 (above 75 threshold), Bollinger %B at 0.92 (near upper band at 194.32), and Z-score (120) at 1.63. Crucially, a confirmed bearish RSI divergence exists on the hourly timeframe — price made a new hourly high while RSI rolled over — which is the reversal signal required before entering a fade. Supplementary bearish evidence: daily OBV is in a downtrend and diverging from price (bearish distribution), daily ADX shows -DI (29.97) > +DI (21.31) confirming the underlying trend direction is still bearish, and the 21-day ROC is -8.78% showing significant medium-term momentum deterioration. Daily MACD bullish crossover is only barely positive (histogram 0.0069) — not convincing. Price has now fulfilled that $192 rally target exactly (currently $193.31). The current data confirms the wiki scenario — no contradiction. Confidence is capped at 58 because the daily Z-score is not at the >2 threshold and the daily RSI-14 is mid-range, so this is an hourly-only extreme rather than a dual-timeframe statistical blow-off.

WMT.reasoning: Neither branch fires. Branch A check: daily Z-score (50) is 1.0 (below the +2 threshold), RSI-14 at 46.79 (neutral), Bollinger %B at 0.596 (mid-range) — no statistical extreme at the daily level. Hourly RSI-21 is 70.29 (approaching but below the 75 threshold) and hourly Z-score (120) is 1.76 (approaching but below 2.0). No RSI divergence detected on either timeframe. Branch A does not trigger. Branch B check: the daily EMAs are aligned in an uptrend (confirmed), and there was a clean pullback from the recent swing high of $132.46 down to $125.91 (a 5.7% move over four days), which touched near the 50% daily Fibonacci retracement at $125.24. However, that Fibonacci entry opportunity has already passed — price has already snapped back sharply to $131.93 (hourly current price) and is now near the prior swing high, not at a Fib confluence entry. Entering Branch B at the current price near $131-132 would put the stop below the 61.8% Fib ($123.54), representing a 6%+ downside risk with minimal room to the resistance at $132.46, yielding a poor risk-reward. Volume on the pullback was not declining (16-17M shares on down days vs 16.5M avg) — a warning flag for Branch B per strategy rules. Wiki context noted WMT needed a clean close above $129 resistance; current data shows price has cleared $129 (contradicting the wiki's caution), but the entry window for a fresh Fib pullback buy has closed. ADX daily remains weak at 12.89, which further undermines any Branch B conviction. Output is neutral: the pullback-to-Fib entry was a valid setup that has already played out, and no current extreme exists to fade.

CVX.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q1 2026 earnings are imminent (April 24-May 1 window; today is April 30, so earnings are either today/tomorrow or just reported). The setup is a classic pre-catalyst exhaustion/sell-the-news risk. Net news sentiment: mixed-negative. Wells Fargo upgraded to $222 post-April 8 selloff (positive), but the Q1 print is expected to reflect $2.7-3.7B in Middle East commodity damage with consensus EPS at $1.50 — a low bar that may still disappoint on revenue/production. Oil ceasefire has already reversed the energy risk premium. Insider activity: net selling — insider Pate sold $8.6M; no offsetting buys. Technical setup going in: extended and deteriorating. Hourly RSI at 76.18 (overbought), hourly RSI bearish divergence confirmed, price at 92% of hourly Bollinger band, daily OBV trend is DOWN and diverging from price (bearish accumulation/distribution). Daily ADX 32.24 but minus_di (29.97) exceeds plus_di (21.31), indicating bears still control the daily directional trend despite the recent 3-day bounce. The bounce from $183.67 to $193 has occurred without volume confirmation (relative volume 0.77, below average). Z-score 120 at 1.63 on the hourly signals short-term overextension. Wiki confirms the structural thesis: the ceasefire ended the Hormuz disruption premium; the upcoming earnings will crystallize the $2.7-3.7B hit. Analyst consensus at $206 provides a floor, but the near-term catalyst is bearish. Selling into the earnings event with the extended technical setup is the higher-probability trade.
